items.title: Iraq, Syria Move to Restore Strategic Kirkuk-Baniyas Oil Pipeline
items.desc: The Iraq-Syria agreement to rehabilitate the Kirkuk-Baniyas pipeline could expand Iraqi export routes, restore Syria's role as an energy transit corridor, and strengthen regional energy connectivity.

items.title: Kurdistan Exports Onions Internationally for First Time, Marking Agricultural Milestone
items.desc: The first international shipment of 3,000 tons of Kurdistan-grown onions to Türkiye underscores the Region's broader strategy to expand agricultural exports, strengthen farmers' access to new markets, and diversify the economy.

items.title: KRG Expands Water Infrastructure as Ninth Cabinet Advances Long-Term Water Security
items.desc: Large-scale investments in potable water production, distribution networks, and wastewater treatment are reshaping the Kurdistan Region's long-term approach to water security and sustainable development.

items.title: Iraq court records confessions in Al-Yamani marriage cases
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad A court in Baghdad has ratified the confessions of two defendants accused of belonging to the Ahmadi Religion of Peace and Light (AROPL), locally known as the Al-Yamani Movement, Iraq's Supreme Judicial Council said on Sunday. items.title: Iraq arrests 5K+ in 2025 human trafficking cases
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraqi authorities detained 5,107 people in human trafficking investigations during 2025, but only 385 victims were formally identified, the Iraqi Observatory for Human Rights (IOHR) revealed on Sunday. items.title: Gold prices retreat in Baghdad and Erbil markets
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad/ Erbil On Sunday, gold prices hovered around 86 0,000 IQD per mithqal in Baghdad and Erbil markets, according to a survey by Shafaq News Agency. Gold prices on Baghdad's Al-Nahr Street recorded a selling price of 85 6,000 IQD per mithqal (equivalent to five grams) for 21-carat gold, including Gulf, Turkish, and European varieties, with a buying price of 852,000 IQD. items.title: Gaza: Israeli attacks leave two dead, one injured
Dollar falls in Baghdad, Erbil markets
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad/ Erbil The US dollar opened Sunday’s trading lower in Iraq, hovering around 153,000 dinars per 100 dollars. According to Shafaq News market survey, the dollar traded in Baghdad's Al-Kifah and Al-Harithiya exchanges at 152,600 dinars per 100 dollars, down from the previous session ’s 153,250 dinars. items.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park

items.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.desc: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

items.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
items.desc: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

items.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
items.desc: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.
